mnemonics.title: My Java Resources Build Great Apps
mnemonics.content: M-J-R-B-G-A: Manifest (manifests/), Java (java/), Resources (res/), Build (build.gradle), Gradle (gradle/), App (app/). Remember the core directories through this sequence

mnemonics.title: Gradle Root Always Directs Structure
mnemonics.content: GRADS: Gradle files (gradle/), Root config (build.gradle), App module (app/), Directories (manifests/, java/, res/), Settings (settings.gradle). Helps recall root-level components

mnemonics.title: Test Placement Matters
mnemonics.content: Remember test directories with 'A-Test Below, Unit Beside': androidTest in parallel to main, test directory at same level as main source

mnemonics.title: Resource Categories Sequence
mnemonics.content: DLMV Mansion: Drawable (drawable/), Layout (layout/), Mipmap (mipmap/), Values (values/). Remember res/ subdirectories in order of frequency of use

acronyms.expansion: Gradle Rules All Development Lifecycle Elements
acronyms.usage: Remember Gradle's role in build automation and project configuration
Recall test directories: (test) for unit tests, (androidTest) for instrumented tests
Key resource directories in res/ folder

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a tree: Roots (project root), Trunk (app/), Branches (manifests/, java/, res/), Leaves (individual files)
Picture a blueprint document with app icon, permissions, and activity list as floor plans
Visualize a toolbox (gradle/ directory) containing identical tools for all developers
See res/ as a department store: Layouts (1st floor), Drawables (2nd floor), Values (basement storage)

analogies.analogy: Like a company HQ: build.gradle is the CEO (global rules), settings.gradle is the HR manager (team structure), gradle/ is the IT department (tools distribution)
Think of it as a smartphone factory: manifests/ (product specs), java/ (assembly line), res/ (raw materials), build.gradle (factory settings)
Like a kitchen pantry: Drawables (spices in jars), Layouts (recipe books), Values (measuring cups) - everything in its designated place
